    "text": "Some properties of the operation of intersection\n(i)\nA  ∩ B = B ∩ A\n(ii)\n(A  ∩ B) ∩ C = A  ∩ (B ∩ C)\n(iii)\nφ ∩ A = φ ; U ∩ A = A\n(iv)\nA ∩ A = A\n(v)\nA ∩ (B ∪ C) = (A ∩ B) ∪ (A ∩ C)\n(vi)\nA ∪ (B ∩ C) = (A ∪ B) ∩ (A ∪ C)\nDifference of sets  The difference of two sets A and B, denoted by A – B is defined\nas set of elements which belong to A but not to B. We write\nA – B = {x : x ∈ A and x ∉ B}\nalso,\nB – A = { x : x ∈ B and x ∉A}\nComplement of a set  Let U be the universal set and A a subset of U. Then the\ncomplement of A is the set of all elements of U which are not the elements of A.\nSymbolically, we write\nA′ = {x : x ∈ U and x ∉ A}. Also A′ = U – A",

(2)\nFrom (1) and (2), (A – B) ∩ (C – B) =  (A ∩ C) – B\nExample 11  Let A, B and C be sets. Then show that\nA ∪ (B ∩ C) = (A ∪ B) ∩ (A ∪ C)\nSolution  We first show that A ∪ (B ∩ C) ⊂ (A ∪ B) ∩ (A ∪ C)\nLet x ∈ A ∪ (B ∩ C). Then\nx ∈ A\nor\nx ∈ B ∩ C\n⇒\nx ∈ A\nor\n(x ∈ B and x ∈ C)\n⇒\n(x ∈ A or x ∈ B) and (x ∈ A or x ∈ C)\n⇒\n(x ∈ A ∪ B) and\n(x ∈ A ∪ C)\n⇒\nx ∈ (A ∪ B) ∩ (A ∪ C)\nThus,\nA ∪ (B ∩ C) ⊂ (A ∪ B) ∩ (A ∪ C)\n... (1)\nNow we will show that (A ∪ B) ∩ (A ∪ C) ⊂ (A ∪ C)\nLet\nx ∈ (A ∪ B) ∩ (A ∪ C)\n⇒\nx ∈ A ∪ B and x ∈ A ∪ C\n⇒\n(x ∈ A or x ∈ B) and (x ∈ A or x ∈ C)\n⇒\nx ∈ A or (x ∈ B and x ∈ C)\n⇒\nx ∈ A or (x ∈ B ∩ C)\n⇒\nx ∈ A ∪ (B ∩ C)\nThus,\n(A ∪ B) ∩ (A ∪ C) ⊂ A ∪ (B ∩ C)\n... (2)\nSo, from (1) and (2), we have\nA ∩ (B ∪ C) = (A ∪ B) ∩ (A ∪ C)",
